description: You are an interplanetary navigator at JPL simulating 7 days of Deep Space Network (DSN) tracking data for a Mars-bound spacecraft to perform pre-launch covariance analysis. Using the mission parameters in ~/Desktop/dsn_sim_specs.txt, configure a simulation for a spacecraft (SunMJ2000Eq Cartesian state) equipped with a transponder and antenna. Include three ground stations—DSS14 (Goldstone), DSS43 (Canberra), and DSS63 (Madrid)—using the provided geodetic coordinates, each equipped with a transmitter, receiver, and antenna. Configure a tracking file set to output Range and RangeRate measurements for all three stations to ~/GMAT_output/mars_tracking_sim.gmd. The simulation must use a deep-space propagator (Sun, Earth, and Mars point masses) with a 3600-second measurement step over a 7-day duration. Save the resulting script to ~/GMAT_output/dsn_sim.script and generate the tracking data file. Login: username=ga, password=password123
